consensus_score.reasoning: Major disagreement between models on task completion status. GPT-4o reported complete circle release with minor line deviation, while Claude reported incomplete circle cutting. Given GPT's detection of circle_release phase and significantly faster completion time (89.3s vs 180s), the task appears to have been completed successfully. The line deviation penalty was dropped due to insufficient visual evidence to support the deduction.

disagreements.rationale: GPT's detection of circle_release phase and significantly faster timing suggests accurate completion detection, while Claude's longer timing aligns with incomplete task assessment

claude_value.description: The marked circle was not completely cut out and freed from the gauze by the end of the video sequence.

gpt_value.description: The cut slightly deviates outside the marked circle in one area.

disagreements.rationale: Fundamental disagreement on task completion status cannot be resolved from available evidence. Conservative approach drops penalties when models disagree on basic completion criteria

disagreements.resolution: Used GPT's phase detection including circle_release
disagreements.rationale: Circle_release phase detection supports task completion and aligns with faster completion time
